stories.title: The Sunbelt and Multifamily: Oversupply Isn’t the Whole Story
stories.excerpt: Casey MacMaster For the past two years, one word has dominated conversations about the Sunbelt apartment market: oversupply. The numbers behind the narrative are real. A wave of multifamily projects that broke ground during the pandemic construction boom came online between 2023 and 2025, flooding many fast-growing markets throughout the Southwest and Southeast with new inventory. Rent growth slowed, concessions increased, and occupancy rates declined, fueling headlines that painted the entire region as overbuilt. But industry experts told Connect CRE that the reality is more nuanced. While the metrics are accurate, treating the Sunbelt as a single apartment market overlooks the differences in local economies, demand, demographics and market fundamentals that determine whether new supply is a problem or a temporary growing pain. “I would not say the supply numbers themselves are exaggerated,” said Casey MacMaster, Bonaventure’s senior vice president, investment and portfolio manager. “The numbers are what they are. The consideration is how the narrative is framed.” More Than a Market One of the largest problems with the oversupply narrative is classifying the Sunbelt as a single entity. It’s not. It’s a broad geographic label covering roughly 15 to 18 states stretching from Southern California across the Southwest and Gulf Coast to the Southeast. Those states have different economies, regulatory environments and business climates. Texas, Florida, Georgia and Tennessee consistently rank among CNBC’s top states for business, while Mississippi and Louisiana rank near the bottom. California’s economy is driven by technology, entertainment and aerospace, while South Carolina continues to attract advanced manufacturing and industrial investment. Ashley Stefan These different jobs economies attract different populations. And those populations drive apartment demand. “The biggest misconception is that the Sunbelt is a single,
bullets: Sunbelt apartment oversupply narrative treats 15–18 state region as single market, obscuring local demand variation
Pandemic construction boom (2023–2025 deliveries) caused rent slowdown, concessions, occupancy declines across Southwest/Southeast
Industry experts warn headline masks submarket fundamentals; supply is real but context-dependent, not uniformly problematic
stories.summary: The Sunbelt multifamily oversupply story is accurate in raw numbers—a wave of pandemic-era construction flooded fast-growing markets across the Southwest and Southeast, slowing rent growth, raising concessions, and depressing occupancy. But lumping 15–18 states together obscures critical differences in local economies, demographics, and demand drivers. Experts argue the narrative conflates a temporary absorption challenge with structural overbuilding.
stories.impact: DEMAND AND DEVELOPMENT STRATEGY: Sunbelt-focused developers must disaggregate risk by submarket, not region. Markets with strong job growth, inbound migration, and limited pre-pandemic supply will absorb 2023–2025 deliveries faster; weak submarkets may face years of pressure. Window to underwrite selective fill-in and infill projects in micro-markets with genuine demand tailwinds (tech corridors, logistics hubs) while avoiding widespread field starts.

stories.title: Rivani Sells Wynwood Jungle Retail Building in Miami for $26M
stories.excerpt: Prominent real estate investor Robert Rivani has sold the Wynwood Jungle retail building in Miami for $25.7 million, property records show. Tabani Group purchased the 39,000-square-foot property between 75 and 43 Northwest 23rd Street, west of North Miami Avenue. The deal is equal to almost $660 per square foot. Tenants on the ground floor include Häagen-Dazs and doughnut purveyor The Salty. Fabel, an upscale restaurant on the rooftop, recently shuttered. About half of the leasable space remains available, according to a listing. Frost Bank supplied a $15.7 million mortgage to the Dallas-based buyer, an investor specialized in retail and multifamily asset classes. Rivani purchased the property for $13.3 million in 2021. Under his ownership, Wynwood Jungle has been embroiled in two lawsuits. One involved a steakhouse from Goat Hospitality owner Derek Gonzalez that ultimately did not open. Rivani sued this year, alleging he is owed $9.1 million in back rent, Business Journals reported. Restaurateur Brian Swanson sued, claiming the landlord provided only 6,158 square feet, instead of 7,751 square feet stipulated in the lease, The Real Deal reported. Rivani contended that the suit was a ploy to break the lease. The transaction represents the latest sale for Rivani, who has been offloading retail properties typically after signing leases with high-end restaurants. Last year, the investor sold the space housing Bad Bunny’s Gekko in Brickell, and Amara at Paraiso in Edgewater and Catch in Miami Beach for $86 million. Representatives for Tabani Group and Rivani did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Julia Echikson can be reached at jechikson@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: Rivani sold Wynwood Jungle retail (39K sf) to Tabani Group for $25.7M, $660/sf; 50% of leasable space vacant
Building saddled with tenant litigation (two lawsuits), high turnover (Fabel restaurant closed), and landlord-tenant disputes
Wynwood Miami retail market signals: ground-floor food/beverage volatility, occupancy risk for mixed-use retail plays in tight urban nodes
stories.summary: Robert Rivani offloaded the Wynwood Jungle retail property (39,000 sf) to Tabani Group for $25.7M ($660/sf) after the building accumulated two active lawsuits involving tenants and a failed steakhouse opening. The property is 50% vacant. Tabani Group, a retail/multifamily investor, acquired it with $15.7M mortgage from Frost Bank.
stories.impact: CAPITAL AND COST: Wynwood retail faces occupancy and tenant-mix challenges; the $660/sf price and 50% vacancy signal distress. For multifamily developers considering Wynwood infill or mixed-use, this flags neighborhood retail fragility and tenant risk—important for ground-floor activation assumptions in proformas. Retail headwinds may depress blended rents and lease-up timelines.

stories.title: Ascendant Capital Partners Acquires Virginia Hotel Portfolio
stories.excerpt: Ascendant Capital Partners has acquired an eight-hotel portfolio from Coastal Hospitality Associates, LLC. The Portfolio comprises 965 predominantly beachfront rooms across six hotels in Virginia Beach and two hotels in the Outer Banks. Ascendant will seek to implement targeted capital and operational enhancements across the portfolio, while Schulte Hospitality Group will assume operations. The portfolio consists of SpringHill Suites by Marriott Virginia Beach Oceanfront, Residence Inn by Marriott Virginia Beach Town Center, Residence Inn by Marriott Virginia Beach Oceanfront, Fairfield by Marriott Inn & Suites Virginia Beach Oceanfront, Holiday Inn VA Beach-Oceanside, Holiday Inn Express & Suites VA Beach Oceanfront, Holiday Inn Express Nags Head Oceanfront, and Holiday Inn Express Kitty Hawk – Outer Banks. CBRE Hotels served as the sell-side advisor to Coastal Hospitality, and CBRE Debt & Structured Finance served as the financing advisor to Ascendant. “This transaction reflects the continued execution of our differentiated hospitality strategy, which is focused on high-quality assets in dynamic, supply-constrained markets with resilient demand drivers,” said Alex Halpern, Co-Founder and Chief Investment Officer of Ascendant. The post Ascendant Capital Partners Acquires Virginia Hotel Portfolio appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Ascendant Capital Partners acquired 8-hotel portfolio (965 rooms) across Virginia Beach, Outer Banks; supply-constrained coastal markets
Portfolio comprises beachfront Marriott/IHG-branded properties; Schulte Hospitality Group to manage; capital play on supply constraints
Demonstrates capital flowing to high-barrier-to-entry coastal markets with limited new room supply and resilient demand
stories.summary: Ascendant Capital Partners bought an eight-hotel portfolio (965 rooms) spanning Virginia Beach and the Outer Banks, anchored by Marriott and IHG brands. Schulte Hospitality Group will manage operations. Ascendant intends to apply capital improvements and operational enhancements, targeting supply-constrained coastal markets with stable demand.
stories.impact: SUPPLY AND DEMAND SIGNAL: Coastal hospitality remains attractive to capital because new room supply is geographically and regulatory constrained. For multifamily developers, this underscores that high-barrier markets (beachfront, zoning-limited) support premium pricing and capital returns. Consider how multifamily supply in Virginia Beach and Outer Banks compares—if similarly constrained, rental demand will remain resilient despite Sunbelt oversupply headlines.

stories.title: New York People and Company News, Week of July 10, 2026
stories.excerpt: MetLife Investment Management, the institutional asset management business of MetLife, Inc., announced that Chris Aiken has been appointed head of Real Estate Equity Strategies. Aiken will lead MIM’s direct property investment platform and oversee investing activities on behalf of MetLife’s general account and third-party institutional investors across core, core-plus, build-to-core, value-add and opportunistic strategies. Newmark Group, Inc. has hired Munish Viralam as Executive Vice Chairman to lead its Real Estate Strategy & Consulting Group. The practice will work alongside Newmark’s advisors to support clients navigating commercial real estate transactions and strategic decisions, including financial analysis, market assessments, operational considerations, lease negotiations and structuring. Based in New York, Viralam leads the group that combines Newmark’s New York Consulting and Financial Services teams. Tyler Caron has joined the Colliers New York Capital Markets team as an associate. Based in New York City, Caron further supports the continued growth of Colliers’ New York office and expands the firm’s capital markets capabilities throughout the region. Cushman & Wakefield announced that Dr. Benjamin Westrich, PhD, has joined the firm as Head of Forecasting. In this role, Westrich will lead the development of the firm’s forecasting capabilities across the Americas, overseeing models, methodologies and systems that support economic outlooks and predictive analytics. PREMIER Design + Build Group has appointed Paul Moser as SVP, Eastern Region. Moser will directly oversee the company’s project portfolio across New Jersey and Pennsylvania, operating out of the firm’s Morristown, nj office. Moser brings more than a decade’s worth of development and construction expertise to PREMIER, having delivered in excess of 6 million square feet of industrial developments and complex redevelopments throughout the region. RIPCO Real Estate app

stories.title: Report: Seattle Office Leasing Activity Exceeds 2.4M SF in Q2 2026
stories.excerpt: The Seattle area office market continued to gain momentum in Q2 2026, with quarterly leasing activity reaching 2.4 million square feet, surpassing Q1 levels and bringing first-half activity to 4.6 million square feet, according to a recent report by Savills. This marked the strongest quarterly leasing performance since early 2019 and the first time since then that the market recorded two consecutive quarters exceeding 2.0 million square feet of leasing volume. Leasing remained concentrated among larger occupiers, with a mix of renewals, relocations and new leases across multiple industries. Technology and artificial intelligence users are expected to remain key drivers of regional leasing activity. The Bellevue central business district remained the region’s highest-priced submarket at $62.34 per square foot, followed by the Seattle central business district at $48.24 per square foot. Tenant preference for newer, amenitized buildings continues, and the ongoing decline in available space suggests market conditions are gradually stabilizing as occupiers make longer-term real estate decisions. The post Report: Seattle Office Leasing Activity Exceeds 2.4M SF in Q2 2026 appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Seattle office leasing 2.4M sf Q2 2026, strongest quarter since early 2019; two consecutive 2M+ sf quarters achieved
Tech/AI tenants driving demand; Bellevue CBD $62.34/sf, Seattle CBD $48.24/sf; tenant preference for newer, amenitized space
Available space declining; market stabilizing as occupiers make longer-term decisions; demand concentration among larger occupiers
stories.summary: Seattle office market rebounded to 2.4M sf leasing in Q2 2026, the strongest quarter since early 2019 and marking the first time since then that two consecutive quarters exceeded 2M sf. Tech and AI users are key drivers. Tenant demand remains concentrated among larger occupiers seeking newer, amenitized buildings. Available space is declining, signaling market stabilization.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY: Strong office leasing in Seattle reflects job growth and occupier confidence, particularly in tech/AI sectors. This directly supports multifamily demand from in-migrating workers and companies establishing regional hubs. Seattle multifamily developers should expect sustained job-growth tailwinds in 2027 and beyond. Bellevue and Seattle CBD office strength suggests parallel multifamily supply constraints and rent upside in proximate neighborhoods; validate local apartment supply pipeline against job creation.

His new role will see him relocate from San Francisco to Los Angeles, where he will commence with earning his master’s in real estate development degree from USC while working from the firm’s El Segundo office. RETS Associates, one of the nation’s leading executive and permanent search firms focused exclusively on the commercial real estate (CRE) industry, announced that industry veteran Christina Truong has joined the firm as a Director in Los Angeles. Truong brings more than a decade of experience spanning recruiting, operations, and advisory services within the real estate industry. Most recently,, she served as a Managing Director of Operations, leading recruiting efforts for a leading real estate technology firm.  Partner Engineering and Science, Inc. hired Adrienne Perez as Technical Director, Agency Environmental Services. Based in Atlanta, Perez will provide technical leadership, quality assurance, and compliance oversight for environmental due diligence services supporting Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac multifamily transactions nationwide.   SRS Real Estate Partners has expanded its California Industrial footprint with the opening of a new office in Greater Los Angeles and the addition of industrial real estate professionals Chris McKenzie and Jason Sneed. McKenzie joins SRS as VP, bringing more than a decade of industrial real estate experience. Before joining SRS, McKenzie was a principal at Lee & Associates, where he consistently ranked among the firm’s leading producers. Sneed joins SRS as an Associate. He previously worked alongside McKenzie at Lee & Associates.   The BLVD Group, a leading multifamily investment and development firm, appointed Estelle Chan as Director of Acquis

stories.title: Nvidia Signs Downtown D.C. Office Lease as Trophy Buildings Outperform
stories.excerpt: The most valuable company in the country is expanding its presence in the nation’s capital. NVIDIA signed an office lease in Washington, D.C., to become the latest technology company to take space in the District while demand concentrates in the market’s premier buildings. The artificial intelligence chipmaker signed a 27,600-square-foot sublease at Douglas Development’s Woodies Building at 1025 F Street NW, according to Cushman & Wakefield’s second-quarter market report. The space is being subleased from Morning Consult, which leased the building’s entire 50,000-square-foot eighth floor in 2020, according to Bisnow. Nvidia also maintains an office in Herndon, Va. The new Nvidia lease adds another high-profile tenant to the Woodies Building, where Netflix is also opening a 24,000-square-foot office and entertainment space. The building has simultaneously been backfilling space previously occupied by the FBI, whose roughly 200,000-square-foot lease expired last year. Nvidia also reinforces a trend of tech firms expanding in Downtown Washington, which has seen CoreWeave, SpaceX and potentially Google taking new deals. Washington’s office recovery remains uneven while its premier buildings have separated themselves from the rest of the market. Cushman & Wakefield reported trophy office vacancy finished the quarter at just 13.4 percent, while overall vacancy climbed to 23.3 percent in the second quarter. Gross leasing totaled 1.6 million square feet during the quarter, and 2.9 million square feet during the first half, down roughly 12 percent from the same period last year. Gregory Cornfield can be reached at gcornfield@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: Nvidia signed 27.6K-sf sublease at Woodies Building (1025 F St NW), Washington D.C.; trophy office outperforming weaker stock
Netflix also leasing 24K sf in same building; FBI 200K-sf lease lapsed; tech firms expanding Downtown DC despite broader office malaise
Downtown DC high-quality assets attract major tenants (Nvidia, Netflix); backfill of trophy space signals selective market strength
stories.summary: Nvidia signed a 27,600-sf sublease at the Woodies Building in Downtown Washington D.C., subleased from Morning Consult. Netflix is simultaneously taking 24,000 sf in the same building. The tower is backfilling space vacated by the FBI (200K sf lease expiration). These moves underscore that trophy buildings and high-amenity space in tech hubs continue to attract major occupiers despite office sector headwinds.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY: Nvidia and Netflix expansion in Downtown DC validates job growth in AI and media/tech sectors. This drives multifamily demand in DC proper and surrounding submarkets (Arlington, Alexandria, Bethesda). Developers should prioritize transit-adjacent, walkable multifamily near job clusters in trophy office zones. The concentration of demand in premier buildings signals that secondary office will underperform—multifamily near weak office stock faces lower jobs-per-unit yield.

stories.title: Bobby Zar Snaps Up 827 Broadway for $19M Via Short Sale
stories.excerpt: There’s a changing of the guard in Greenwich Village. Bobby Zar’s ZG Capital Partners has snapped up the interconnected buildings at 827-831 Broadway, plus 47 East 12th Street, for $18.5 million via a short sale, sources told Commercial Observer. Newmark’s Brett Siegel, Maurice Suede and Adam Spies negotiated the lender-directed sale on behalf of the seller, while ZG Capital’s Zar and James Tamborlane led the transaction in-house. The buildings, which sit at the eastern edge of Greenwich Village just south of Union Square, sold for less than a third of the price they traded for just over a decade ago, representing a steep haircut. Quality Capital and Caerus purchased the four-story buildings in 2015 for $60 million, with plans to bring a 300-foot, 14-story office and retail building to the address, The Real Deal reported at the time. The plan was to demolish the existing cast-iron structures and build 11 stories of office floors atop three stories of retail. In May 2017, the borrowers recapitalized their investment, taking a $59 million loan from LoanCore Capital. However, in October 2017, following a campaign led by Village Preservation that fought for landmark designation, the New York City Landmarks Preservation Commission put a stop to the developers’ plans, granting landmark status to 827-831 Broadway and preventing the buildings’ demolition due to their significant cultural and historical significance. (The 12th Street building was not designated.) Indeed, the buildings have quite the rich history. In the 19th century, the Broadway buildings were home to the Wilson Sewing Machine Company as well as A.A. Vantine, the first importer of Japanese arts into the U.S. In the 1950s and 1960s, the top floors of the Griffith Thomas-designed, Italianate structures served as studios for prominent artists such as abstract expressionist artist Willem de Kooning, who painted his famous “Pastorale” at 831 Broadway in 1963. It wasn’t immediately clear what the plans are for th
bullets: Bobby Zar/ZG Capital acquired 827–831 Broadway + 47 E 12th St (Greenwich Village) for $18.5M via short sale; 67% haircut from 2015
Quality Capital/Caerus paid $60M in 2015 for office/retail redevelopment; project never delivered, distressed disposition reflects CRE stress
Greenwich Village/Union Square mixed-use value-add play; low entry price signals capital availability for repositioned assets
stories.summary: Bobby Zar's ZG Capital Partners acquired the interconnected buildings at 827–831 Broadway and 47 East 12th Street in Greenwich Village for $18.5M via short sale—roughly 67% below the $60M purchase price set by Quality Capital and Caerus in 2015. The original plan was a 14-story office/retail redevelopment (demolished to 300-foot structure); the project stalled. The short sale reflects distressed CRE capital and landlord inability to execute redevelopment.
stories.impact: CAPITAL AVAILABILITY AND RISK: Distressed sales in prime Manhattan submarkets signal capital is repricing legacy office/retail redevelopment risk and forced sellers. For multifamily developers, this opens acquisition windows for repositioned sites near Union Square/Greenwich Village—strong multifamily demand nodes. However, regulatory and financing risks remain material; validate entitlements and financing appetite before committing. The 2015–2026 value destruction suggests execution risk in complex NYC redevelopment; simpler, shovel-ready multifamily projects outperform.

stories.title: NBCUniversal Renews 244K-SF Offices at 1221 Avenue of the Americas
stories.excerpt: NBCUniversal is keeping its massive office near Rockefeller Center. The mass media and entertainment conglomerate has renewed its 244,185-square-foot lease at Rockefeller Group’s 1221 Avenue of the Americas, according to a second-quarter office report from CBRE. NBC first signed a lease for its space at the 51-story Midtown office building in 2012 as part of a $190 million investment into its New York City operations, according to an announcement at the time. That investment also included a series of upgrades and renovations to NBC’s headquarters across the street at 30 Rockefeller Plaza. The length of the new renewal and the asking rent were unclear, but CBRE’s report found office asking rents in Midtown averaged $86.18 per square foot during the second quarter of 2026. It’s also unclear who brokered the deal. Rockefeller Group declined to comment, while spokespeople for CBRE, NBC and Cushman & Wakefield — which has represented Rockefeller Group in past deals at 1221 Avenue of the Americas — did not immediately respond to requests for comment. NBC last signed a renewal deal at the Midtown property in 2021, when it extended a lease for a secondary office at the building, bringing its overall footprint up to 339,933 square feet. It’s unclear whether the new renewal is only for part of NBC’s office space at the property, or whether NBC reduced its total office footprint there. Nevertheless, news of NBC’s new deal comes during a busy period for office renewals in Manhattan. And those renewals happen to be some of the largest leases signed during the second quarter, according to CBRE’s report. Down at One Liberty Plaza in the Financial District, AON, a global professional services firm that provides risk, retirement and health solutions, renewed 201,931 square feet, the report found. Meanwhile, over in Hudson Square, WNYC Radio, New York City’s flagship public radio station, signed a renewal for 87,283 square feet at 160 Varick Street, according to CBRE. Overall, Ma
bullets: NBCUniversal renewed 244.2K-sf lease at 1221 Avenue of the Americas (Rockefeller Group), Midtown Manhattan; first lease signed 2012
Midtown office asking rents averaged $86.18/sf in Q2 2026; major media tenants anchoring trophy buildings despite office sector headwinds
Renewal signals long-term occupier commitment in high-rent core; trophy Midtown space remains premium despite macro uncertainty
stories.summary: NBCUniversal renewed its 244,185-sf lease at 1221 Avenue of the Americas (Rockefeller Group) in Midtown Manhattan. The company first signed the lease in 2012 as part of a $190M New York investment. Midtown office rents averaged $86.18/sf in Q2 2026. Renewal length and rate were not disclosed, but the deal underscores continued occupancy of trophy buildings by blue-chip media tenants.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY: NBC's renewal in Midtown signals sustained large-employer presence and job stability in Manhattan's core. Media/broadcasting is a local economic pillar. Strong Midtown office demand will support multifamily demand in adjacent neighborhoods (Upper West Side, Hells Kitchen, Midtown East residential). However, rent concentration in trophy stock means secondary Manhattan multifamily faces moderate headwinds from office-to-residential conversion pressure and limited job growth outside trophy corridors. Focus on trophy-adjacent multifamily with strong walkability and transit access.

stories.title: Heart to Heart Homecare Takes 13K SF for Office in South Bronx
stories.excerpt: Heart to Heart Homecare, a home health and personal care provider, is bringing its services to the South Bronx. The home care services agency, which provides nonmedical assistance to seniors and individuals with disabilities, has signed a 10-year, 12,500-square-foot lease for a new office at 369 148th Street, Commercial Observer has learned. Asking rent for the space was $38 per square foot, according to a source close to the deal. Nick Zweig from Locations Commercial Real Estate was the sole broker on the deal. Zweig and Heart to Heart Homecare did not immediately respond to Commercial Observer’s requests for comment. Heart to Heart Homecare provides services in all five boroughs, and offers health care and social services to people who are elderly, disabled and injured. The company also has an office in East Orange, N.J. The four-story South Bronx property, which is owned by Marc Fishman of Edith Court LLC, was built in 1931 and spans 55,500 square feet, according to Loopnet.com. The Bronx Legal Services, Nexus Medical Training Center and the Bronx Peer Advocacy Center are also tenants at 369 148th Street. Amanda Schiavo can be reached at aschiavo@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: Heart to Heart Homecare leased 12.5K sf in South Bronx (369 148th St), 10-year term at $38/sf; health services tenant expansion
Healthcare/homecare agencies targeting Bronx as affordable operational hub for aging/disabled population service; signifies emerging service-sector demand
South Bronx gentrification signal: white-collar service tenants entering historically industrial/distressed neighborhoods; labor-intensive, low-wage job growth
stories.summary: Heart to Heart Homecare, a home health and personal care provider, signed a 10-year, 12,500-sf lease at 369 148th Street in the South Bronx at $38/sf. The company provides nonmedical services to seniors and individuals with disabilities and operates across all five boroughs.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY—INDIRECT: Homecare and elder-services expansion in the Bronx reflects aging population demand and healthcare service growth in outer boroughs. This is a lower-wage, labor-intensive sector, distinct from high-wage job creation. For multifamily, it signals demographic pressures (aging in place, population aging) and workforce demand (homecare workers, nurses, home health aides), but these jobs typically pay $40–$60K and do not support Class A rents. Developers should target workforce/affordable multifamily near South Bronx service clusters; luxury multifamily demand remains limited absent broader economic revitalization or gentrification wave.

stories.title: Vernon Hills clears 1.2M sf industrial redevelopment on former American Hotel Register site
stories.excerpt: Redevelopment of an ex-American Hotel Register property northwest of Chicago was approved,  greenlighting an additional 1 million-square-foot industrial park in the region.  The Vernon Hills site spans 70 acres and is set for 1.2 million square feet spread across three speculative buildings, one existing warehouse and a site for hand2mind, a classroom supplies and teacher curriculum company. An entity listed as JCA Hayes LLC has been trying to get this project in motion since late 2024, according to the Chicago Daily Herald. The planned site sits at 100-230 South Milwaukee Avenue.  At one point, it appeared that the land was […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Vernon Hills, IL approved 1.2M-sf industrial redevelopment on ex-American Hotel Register site (70 acres); three speculative warehouses + hand2mind
Project originally linked to Amazon 'Project Zeus' (3M-sf office/warehouse), ultimately withdrawn; now smaller speculative light industrial play
Chicago suburbs industrial supply growth; conditional approval with sound wall mitigation; classroom supplies firm (hand2mind) consolidating facilities regionally
stories.summary: Vernon Hills approved a 1.2M-sf industrial redevelopment on a 70-acre former American Hotel Register site, including three speculative warehouse buildings and facilities for hand2mind, a classroom and curriculum supplies company. The project, pursued by JCA Hayes LLC since late 2024, was originally suspected to be Amazon's 'Project Zeus' (3M-sf office/warehouse proposal) but was withdrawn years ago. The current approval is conditional, requiring sound wall mitigation for neighboring properties.
stories.impact: SUPPLY AND DEVELOPMENT MARKET: Chicago suburbs continuing industrial sprawl and speculative warehouse development. While this directly affects industrial, it signals regional growth and logistics/e-commerce job creation (lower-wage). Multifamily developers near Vernon Hills and surrounding North Shore/Northwest suburbs should monitor workforce housing demand from logistics and light manufacturing. However, speculative warehouses on greenfield do not drive high-rent multifamily demand; focus on transit-adjacent, urban-core multifamily in Chicago proper or high-barrier submarkets.

stories.title: Caterpillar digs into new $34M HQ expansion in Las Colinas
stories.excerpt: Caterpillar, a worldwide construction and engineering manufacturer that calls suburban Dallas home, is planning an expansion of its headquarters around three miles down the road from its current one at the Towers at Williams Square. The company is expected to spend $36 million to renovate portions of an office property at 901 West Walnut Hill Lane in Irving. Initial phases of the project are slated to cover 107,000 square feet and are expected to be completed around August 2027, according to the Dallas Morning News. The total square footage of the Las Colinas offices is pegged at over 400,000 square […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Caterpillar expanding HQ 107K-sf Phase 1 at Las Colinas (Irving, TX) for $36M; completion Aug 2027; total 400K+ sf potential
Fortune 500 manufacturer consolidating/expanding Dallas footprint post-2022 relocation from Illinois; growing workforce and dealer/customer space
Las Colinas office market receives major employer expansion; signals sustained corporate confidence in North Texas tech/engineering hub
stories.summary: Caterpillar is expanding its Dallas-area headquarters with a $36M renovation of a former Zales/Vari office campus at 901 West Walnut Hill Lane in Irving. Phase 1 covers 107,000 sf and should complete by August 2027, with total buildout potential exceeding 400,000 sf. Caterpillar moved its global HQ to the Dallas-Fort Worth metro from Deerfield, Illinois in 2022 and is now expanding to accommodate workforce growth and customer/dealer space.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY—MAJOR: Caterpillar's continued HQ expansion post-2022 relocation signals sustained corporate commitment and workforce growth in Dallas-Fort Worth. This is a Fortune 500 anchor with global reach, drawing high-wage engineering and corporate talent. Multifamily developers should prioritize supply near Las Colinas and other North Texas office corridors (Uptown Dallas, Plano, Frisco). Strong wage base and employer stability support Class A rents and sustained demand. Validate multifamily pipeline against Caterpillar headcount projections; first-mover advantage to deliver transit-connected, amenity-rich product before competitive saturation.

stories.title: Pendry Nashville enlists Douglas Elliman, broker to woo New York condo buyers
stories.excerpt: A development advisory firm is wagering that a formula crafted in New York combining an intimate live music concert series with philanthropy can draw interest to a high-end Nashville condo project.  Redeavor, the two-year-old firm serving as the exclusive sales and marketing team for the Pendry Residences Nashville, has tapped Douglas Elliman and one of its longtime brokers, Greg Williamson, as New York City marketing partners.  Developed by SomeraRoad, the 30-story, $364 million tower is slated to include 146 condo units on the upper floors, as well as a 180-key hotel. Redeavor’s Maranda Blanton said they anticipate construction will be […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Pendry Residences Nashville, 30-story mixed-use ($364M), 146 condo units + 180-key hotel; $26M presales in Q1, penthouse $7M; units from $1.3M
SomeraRoad developer enlisting Douglas Elliman + NYC broker Greg Williamson to market luxury condos to Northeastern capital; Soho Sessions concert series as draw
Nashville Gulch mixed-use anchored by 278-apartment complex (2024 opening) + 84K sf retail/office; real estate and lifestyle marketing convergence
stories.summary: Pendry Residences Nashville, a 30-story, $364M mixed-use tower developed by SomeraRoad, will include 146 condo units and a 180-key hotel. The project closed $26M in presale condo contracts in Q1 (including a $7M penthouse), with base pricing around $1.3M. SomeraRoad hired Douglas Elliman and NYC broker Greg Williamson to market luxury units to Northeastern buyers, leveraging an invite-only concert series (Soho Sessions). Construction should complete by end of 2027, with hotel opening early 2028.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY—MARKET DIVERGENCE: Pendry is a high-end condo play, not traditional multifamily, but signals sustained luxury capital inflow to Nashville from coastal (NYC) HNW buyers. This reflects Nashville's reputation as lifestyle/entertainment hub and secondary market beneficiary of Northeast coastal migration. For multifamily developers, this validates strong underlying demand for apartments in Nashville core and Gulch. However, condo pre-sales ($1.3M–$7M units) target different buyer profile than rental tenants; developers should focus on Class A/B rental product in same submarkets (transit-adjacent Gulch infill), as apartment demand will benefit from same inbound wealth/talent but at lower entry price point.

stories.title: Miami Freedom Park redevelopment, West Palm office tower among $370M-plus construction permits for week ending July 8, 2026
stories.excerpt: 🏆 New Building: The largest permit issued was a $193 million new construction permit for 1822 Northwest 37th Avenue in Miami, tied to the redevelopment of Miami Freedom Park near the Miami International Airport, which will include more than 1 million square feet of retail, dining, entertainment and office space, 750 hotel rooms, a 58-acre public park, a new City of Miami administration building and Inter Miami CF’s Nu Stadium — which is already complete.Job Filing Number: BD25026033 📊 New Building: The second-largest permit was a $180 million commercial permit for 850 South Quadrille Boulevard in West Palm Beach, where […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Miami Freedom Park redevelopment: $193M permit for 1.82M NW 37th Ave; 1M+ sf retail/dining/entertainment, 750 hotel rooms, 58-acre park, Inter Miami CF Nu Stadium
West Palm Beach: $180M permit for Cohen Brothers office tower (West Palm Point); project stalled since 2020, foreclosure lawsuit 2025; revived post-distress
South Florida mixed-use boom: major entertainment/sports/retail infrastructure draw inbound capital; multifamily infill opportunity in transit corridors
stories.summary: Miami Freedom Park, a major mixed-use redevelopment near Miami International Airport, received a $193M construction permit for 1.82M NW 37th Ave, encompassing 1M+ sf of retail/dining/entertainment, 750 hotel rooms, 58-acre public park, Miami administration building, and Inter Miami CF's Nu Stadium (already complete). West Palm Beach's West Palm Point office tower (Cohen Brothers, $180M permit) revived after years of stalling and a 2025 foreclosure lawsuit. These projects signal capital returning to South Florida mixed-use and office development post-distress.
stories.impact: SUPPLY AND CAPITAL SIGNAL: Miami Freedom Park's scale (1M+ sf retail/entertainment + 750 rooms) will anchor massive foot traffic and employment. Multifamily developers should evaluate nearby infill and rental housing within 1–2 miles—airport employees, retail/hospitality workers, and tourism spillover will drive Class B/C workforce housing demand. West Palm Point's revival from distress suggests capital availability for repositioned office assets, but office headwinds persist; multifamily in adjacent West Palm CBD may benefit from office tenants downsizing and relocating to emerging submarkets. Validate new supply pipeline in both South Florida corridors before committing to new starts.

stories.title: Naterra eyeing a $34M combination office and warehouse in Coppell
stories.excerpt: Naterra International, a cosmetics and beauty company, is looking to expand their operations in Coppell with a new $34 million combination headquarters and warehouse facility.  The facility is expected to span 442,010 square feet, with no additional information on how the space will be split between the office and the warehouse, according to the Dallas Business Journal.  Dallas-based Pross Design Group will be heading up the construction. The future headquarters of the company will sit at 1525 Point West Boulevard in the suburb just northwest of Dallas, and the facility should be completed around December 2027. The price of the […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Naterra International building $34M HQ+warehouse (442K sf) in Coppell, TX; cosmetics/beauty manufacturer consolidating existing Coppell operations at $77/sf
Family-owned beauty supplier (Baby Magic, Tree Hut, Splash brands); 30-year Walmart relationship; facility completion Dec 2027
Dallas-Fort Worth thriving beauty supply/personal care hub; Mary Kay, Sally Beauty, Jack Black, Cetaphil all regional HQ; labor-intensive manufacturing anchor
stories.summary: Naterra International, a family-owned cosmetics and beauty products manufacturer, is building a $34M combined headquarters and warehouse facility (442,010 sf) in Coppell, Texas, with completion targeted for December 2027. The facility costs ~$77/sf and will consolidate Naterra's existing Coppell operations. Naterra supplies brands like Baby Magic, Tree Hut, and Splash to Walmart and other retailers.
stories.impact: DEMAND SIGNAL FOR MULTIFAMILY—REGIONAL: Naterra's continued expansion in Coppell underscores DFW's strength as a beauty/personal care manufacturing and distribution hub. This is a mid-wage, labor-intensive sector (manufacturing, logistics, warehouse operations) supporting workforce housing. Multifamily developers should target Class B/C apartment supply near Coppell and other DFW corporate parks (Plano, Irving, Las Colinas). However, labor intensity and moderate wage profiles mean rental demand is volume-driven and price-sensitive; Class A luxury plays less suitable. Focus on supply near Naterra and peer beauty/care companies to capture workforce housing opportunity.

stories.title: San Antonio closes on three key Project Marvel parcels for $30M
stories.excerpt: San Antonio formally acquired federal properties downtown that are within the boundaries of a potential sports and entertainment district dubbed Project Marvel.  Officials for the General Services Administration and the city announced Monday that San Antonio paid $30 million for the federal office building at 727 East César Chávez Boulevard and two parking lots on Indianola Street. The purchase was funded by the NBA franchise the San Antonio Spurs, city manager Erik Walsh said in a statement. The San Antonio Business Journal reported that part of Project Marvel is set aside for the Spurs, who plan on building a new […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: San Antonio acquired three federal properties for $30M (GSA disposal, Spurs-funded): 727 E César Chávez Blvd + two Indianola St parking lots; Project Marvel downtown
Project Marvel $1.2B–$1.5B mixed-use sports/entertainment district; includes new Spurs arena, convention center expansion, 50-acre mixed-use, 1,000-room hotel
Frost Bank Center (current Spurs home) pivoting to new rodeo district; downtown sports/entertainment infrastructure and public sector land assembly enable private development
stories.summary: San Antonio, funded by the San Antonio Spurs, acquired three federal properties from the GSA for $30M: the federal office building at 727 East César Chávez Boulevard and two parking lots. The purchase advances Project Marvel, a $1.2B–$1.5B downtown sports and entertainment district anchored by a new Spurs arena, expanded Henry B. González Convention Center, 50 acres of mixed-use development, and a 1,000-room hotel. The Frost Bank Center (current Spurs home) is planned for a new rodeo district.
stories.impact: SUPPLY AND DEMAND SIGNAL—MAJOR: Project Marvel represents transformative downtown infill and job/entertainment hub creation. New arena, expanded convention center, and 1,000-room hotel will generate sustained employment and visitor traffic. Multifamily developers should prioritize rental housing within walking distance of the new arena and convention district (downtown core, Southtown, adjacent neighborhoods). This is a decadal catalyst for sustained demand and rents. Validate supply timing against Project Marvel phasing (likely 2027–2030 opening); first-mover advantage for Class A/B product positioned as 'live-work-play' near arena/mixed-use. Strong wage base (professional sports, hospitality, tourism) and demographic tailwinds (Spurs fan base, convention attendees) support premium multifamily valuations.
